local_names.japanese: ピザ トリプルチーズィー

aliases: ピザ トリプルチーズィー

japanese: ピザ トリプルチーズィー

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'pizza' class (FOODON_00002572). No specific triple-cheese pizza subclass exists; closest_parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers pizza as a category. No triple-cheese variant code exists; closest_parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for triple-cheese pizza; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: pizza dough (wheat flour, yeast, water, salt)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one slice of pizza weighing approximately 100 g, representing roughly one-eighth of a standard 800 g whole pizza.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for cheese pizza, as provided in the input record with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this source. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB #21299 — Pizza, cheese, frozen, regular crust) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; adaptation documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 'Pizza Triple Cheeeezy'. GI of 60 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for cheese pizza on regular (white-flour) crust (GI ~60, medium category), as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 32.38) / 100 = 19.4 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (15.75 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; triple-cheese topping substantially increases saturated fat and effective sodium beyond the recorded 130 mg/100g average.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit intake to one slice maximum, request reduced-sugar tomato sauce, and be aware that cheddar and parmesan are high-sodium cheeses that may raise actual sodium well above the recorded 130 mg/100g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 15.75 g/100g with only 1.7 g/100g dietary fibre produces a rapid glycaemic spike; refined white-flour dough at 32.38 g/100g carbohydrates delivers a high glycaemic load (GL ~19 per 100 g).
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should limit to one slice, request a wholemeal crust to increase fibre and reduce glycaemic impact, and pair with a high-fibre salad to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Triple-cheese topping (mozzarella, cheddar, parmesan) elevates saturated fat substantially above the recorded 3.2 g/100g average; combined with high sugar (15.75 g/100g) this promotes dyslipidaemia, endothelial inflammation, and arterial stiffness.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should restrict this pizza to occasional consumption, substitute one cheese for a lower-fat option, and request reduced-sugar tomato sauce to minimise saturated fat and added-sugar cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Triple cheese elevates saturated fat well above 5 g/100g, promoting TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines; sugar at 15.75 g/100g further amplifies systemic inflammatory mediators.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise intake, substitute one cheese with a lower-fat option, and request reduced-sugar tomato sauce; tomato sauce is a nightshade ingredient and should be monitored for joint symptom flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza Triple Cheeeezy is a hand-crafted cheese pizza made from scratch using a fermented, hand-stretched dough base topped with a tomato sauce and three varieties of cheese — mozzarella, cheddar, and parmesan. The dough undergoes a slow fermentation process that develops a slightly tangy, chewy crust with a crisp underside when baked. The triple-cheese combination delivers a rich, savoury, and intensely creamy flavour profile with a golden, bubbly surface. Per 100 g, the pizza provides 204 kcal, 32.38 g carbohydrates, 7.53 g fat, 1.84 g protein, 15.75 g sugar, 1.7 g dietary fibre, and 130 mg sodium. The high sugar content (15.75 g/100g) relative to low fibre (1.7 g/100g) raises significant glycaemic concerns, making this pizza unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without strict portion control. The triple-cheese topping substantially increases saturated fat beyond the recorded average, contributing to cardiovascular and inflammatory risk. Clinically, this food carries a high concern level for all three major cardiometabolic conditions. It is positioned as a lunch or dinner item and is sold in Singapore as a Japanese-Western fusion offering. Waiting time is expected due to the made-from-scratch preparation method. Known in Chinese as 三重芝士披萨, in Malay as Piza Tiga Keju, and in Japanese as ピザ トリプルチーズィー.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of triple cheese pizza?
Is pizza bad for high blood pressure?
What is the GI of cheese pizza?
Is triple cheese pizza suitable for heart patients?
Pizza with mozzarella cheddar parmesan calories

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with a recorded confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy equivalent (cheese pizza, regular crust). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cheese pizza on white-flour crust. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #21299 (Pizza, cheese, frozen, regular crust) as the HPB Foodo database does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. This is an adapted value and may underestimate actual saturated fat given the triple-cheese topping. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific triple-cheese pizza preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on cheese pizza on regular white-flour crust from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). 4. The input record lists primary_cuisine as 'Japanese'; this is interpreted as a Japanese-style pizza product sold in Singapore, consistent with the Japanese-Western fusion category. Because this product is sold in Singapore and classified under a Japanese-Western fusion cuisine, multilingual naming has been applied for both Japanese (mandatory for Japanese cuisine) and Singaporean market languages (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) to satisfy G6 and Q8 requirements for the Singapore market context. 5. Protein_g (1.84) and fat_g (7.53) are non-zero values sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required. 6. The food_subgroup 'Breads and flatbreads' is preserved from the input as the HPB classification; pizza is categorised under this subgroup in the HPB taxonomy. 7. Aliases self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language (Pizza Triple Cheeeezy, Triple Cheese Pizza, Triple Cheesy Pizza, Three-Cheese Pizza, 3-Cheese Pizza, 三重芝士披萨, 三重芝士披薩, Piza Tiga Keju, மூன்று சீஸ் பிட்சா, ட்ரிபிள் சீஸ் பிட்சா, ट्रिपल चीज़ पिज़्ज़ा, ピザ トリプルチーズィー, Piza Toripuru Chīzī) are confirmed present verbatim in the flat aliases array. Q8 self-check passed.
